threats.title: Identity impersonation
threats.description: An attacker impersonates an Admin, Professor, or Student using stolen credentials, session hijacking, or weak authentication mechanisms.
threats.mitigation: Use strong password policies and implement secure session management

threats.title: Elevation of Privilege
threats.description: A normal user exploits broken access control or insecure role validation to gain admin-level permissions and perform restricted actions
threats.mitigation: Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with strict server-side enforcement and apply principle of least privilege for all roles and validate authorization on every request

threats.title: Information disclosure
threats.description: Sensitive user data (emails, roles, account status, hashed passwords, or internal metadata) is exposed via insecure APIs, logs, or over-permissive queries.
threats.mitigation: Apply data minimization (only return necessary fields in APIs) and  enforce strict API authorization checks per endpoint and object

threats.title: Identity impersonation
threats.description: An attacker impersonates a Professor or Student to access course creation, enrollment, or viewing features without authorization
threats.mitigation: Use strong password policies and implement secure session management

threats.title: Unauthorized modification of course data
threats.description: Malicious users modify course content, enrollment lists, or class sessions
threats.mitigation: Enforce server-side validation for all course updates and apply Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) strictly (only Professors/Admins can modify courses) and use audit logs for all course changes

threats.title: Information Disclosure
threats.description: Students or unauthorized users gain access to restricted course data
threats.mitigation: Enforce fine-grained authorization checks per course and user role and apply data filtering at API level (never rely on frontend filtering) and ensure secure API endpoints with least-privilege access

threats.title: Denying Modification
threats.description: A professor denies creating or modifying a course or session
threats.mitigation:
Maintain immutable audit logs (append-only logging system)
Include user ID, timestamp, and action type in logs

threats.title: Denial of Service(DoS)
threats.description: Attackers flood course management endpoints (e.g., enrollment or course listing), making the system unavailable.
threats.mitigation: Implement rate limiting per user/IP and use caching for course listing endpoints

threats.title: Elevation of Privilege
threats.description: A student manipulates requests to gain professor/admin capabilities
threats.mitigation: Strict RBAC enforcement on backend only and validate role claims server-side

threats.title: Identity impersonation
threats.description: An attacker impersonates a Student to submit assignments, or a Professor to grade/modify submissions without authorization
threats.mitigation: Use strong password policies and implement secure session management

threats.title: Submission or grade manipulation
threats.description: Attackers modify submitted assignments, upload altered files, or change grades after submission.
threats.mitigation: Store submissions in immutable or versioned storage and restrict grading actions to authorized professor roles only (

threats.title: Informatio Disclosure
threats.description: Students gain access to other students’ submissions, grades, or confidential feedback. Sensitive files stored in file storage may be exposed.
threats.mitigation: Enforce strict per-user and per-course authorization checks

threats.title: Repudiation
threats.description: A student denies submitting an assignment or a professor denies grading it.
threats.mitigation: Maintain immutable audit logs (submission time, user ID, file hash) and log all grading actions with timestamps + professor identity

threats.title: Denial of Service (DoS)
threats.description: Attackers flood submission endpoints or upload large files to exhaust storage or processing resources.
threats.mitigation: Apply rate limiting on submission endpoints, enforce file upload size limits and use asynchronous processing for uploads (queue-based handling)

threats.title: Elevation of privilege
threats.description: A student attempts to access professor functions (grading, viewing all submissions) or manipulate assignment metadata.
threats.mitigation: Strict RBAC enforced server-side for grading vs submission actions and separate student and professor API endpoints
